entries.value: Ensure the indicator’s color provides at least 3:1 contrast against the surface it's on
Avoid using a color below 3:1 contrast
Always provide a clear ariaLabel that describes the current state of the progress bar. For example, "5 of 10 files uploaded", not "Files uploading".
Wherever possible, also include this text as visual helper text. This lets people know what sub-processes are taking place.
Use a clear success state when the actions required to continue have been fulfilled.
Keep the task concise. Use a maximum of seven steps in your journey so that people aren't overwhelmed.
Use a minimum of 3 steps and keep each step concise.
A progress indicator guides users through any linear, multistep task by showing the user their completed, current, and future steps.
Indicate overall progress of a group of items
Use a stop indicator when placing the progress indicator inside a container with low contrast
Use circular indicators for short, indeterminate activities under 5 seconds

use the built-in label style. Use the built-in style for showing a label associated with the operation. The built-in style always has a left aligned label and a right aligned percentage value above the track.
Use progress circles for loading views
place text close to the progress bar.
Use the progress bar component as a loading indicator or to communicate the status of a system process.
Don’t show the progress of each activity in a group
Avoid applying progress indicators to every button in a list
Do not embed text into the progress bar.
Do not place text far away from the progress bar.
Don't use the progress bar for data visualization. Use a custom component instead.
